authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
USDA Forest Service — Tahoe National Forest

sources.title: CCR T14 §5.85(a)(1) — Trout, General Statewide Regulations (inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

sources.title: CCR T14 §7.40 — Alphabetical List of Hatchery Trout, Hatchery Steelhead, and Salmon Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (French Meadows Reservoir not listed)
CCR T14 §7.50 — Alphabetical List of Trout Waters with Special Fishing Regulations (French Meadows Reservoir not listed)
2026 California Freshwater Sport Fishing Regulations (Updated 06/01/2026)
sources.quotedText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and 10 trout possession limit.

sources.title: California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: French Meadows Reservoir — Recreation Site, Tahoe National Forest, American River Ranger District
sources.quotedText: A CDF&W target fishery for hatchery catchables. Good boat launching facilities and campgrounds. 5,000 lbs of catchable rainbow trout were planted in 2014.

sources.title: French Meadows Reservoir — Placer County Water Agency
sources.quotedText: The Department of Fish and Game stocks French Meadows with rainbows and browns annually.

sources.title: Placer County Water Agency releases 3,000 pounds of rainbow trout into French Meadows Reservoir — FOX40 News (via AOL)
sources.quotedText: Last week, French Meadows Reservoir near Foresthill got a boatload of special visitors — 3,000 pounds of rainbow trout to be exact. ... Generally do it right here before Memorial Day, which was last weekend. Then we'll do it again in mid-July and that usually makes fishing really good for the whole summer.

sources.title: French Meadows Reservoir — Wikipedia
sources.quotedText: French Meadows Reservoir is a manmade lake 36 miles (58 km) northeast of Foresthill, California, United States on the Middle Fork of the American River.

parameters.note: French Meadows Reservoir is not listed in CCR §7.40(b) or §7.50(b), so it falls under the general statewide lake/reservoir regulation in §5.85(a)(1).

regulations.humanSummary: Open all year (general statewide reservoir regulation; not listed as a special-regulation water)
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.counting_rule: General statewide trout limit for inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds: five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily bag limit, 10 trout possession limit
regulations.verbatimText: All inland lakes, reservoirs, and ponds, including those on private lands, are open to fishing all year with a five-trout daily bag limit and ten-trout possession limit.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

parameters.description: French Meadows Reservoir is a CDFW target fishery for hatchery catchables; the reservoir is on Tahoe National Forest land and lies within a State Game Refuge where hunting and discharge of firearms are prohibited. The dam and reservoir are owned and operated by the Placer County Water Agency (PCWA), which supplements CDFW stocking with its own trout plants (roughly twice yearly, before Memorial Day and again in mid-July).

regulations.citation: USFS Tahoe National Forest — French Meadows Reservoir Recreation Site page
regulations.humanSummary: State Game Refuge (no hunting/firearms); reservoir owned/operated by PCWA, which supplements CDFW stocking with its own trout plants
regulations.verbatimText: A CDF&W target fishery for hatchery catchables. Good boat launching facilities and campgrounds. 5,000 lbs of catchable rainbow trout were planted in 2014.

stockingSchedule.note: Placer County Water Agency (dam/reservoir operator) stocks catchable-size rainbow trout roughly twice a year — once before Memorial Day (late May) and again in mid-July — sometimes in coordination with CDFW; a June 2026 release delivered 3,000 pounds of rainbow trout. CDFW's own historical stocking (5,000 lbs of catchable rainbow trout in 2014, per USFS) is not part of the current provisional CDFW stocking plan (French Meadows Reservoir does not appear in CDFW's 2026 Provisional Stocking Document for Trout and Inland Salmon), so this reflects PCWA-driven stocking rather than a guaranteed CDFW annual constant.
